date_from.description: Duration in the past. Supported units are: `h` (hour), `d` (day), `w` (week), `m` (month), `y` (year), `all` (all time). Use the `Start` suffix to define the exact left date boundary. Examples: `-1d` last day from now, `-180d` last 180 days from now, `mStart` this month start, `-1dStart` yesterday's start.

filterTestAccounts.description: Exclude internal and test users by applying the respective filters

key.description: Use one of the properties the user has provided in the plan.

operator.description: `icontains` - case insensitive contains. `not_icontains` - case insensitive does not contain. `regex` - matches the regex pattern. `not_regex` - does not match the regex pattern.

value.description: Only use property values from the plan. If the operator is `regex` or `not_regex`, the value must be a valid ClickHouse regex pattern to match against. Otherwise, the value must be a substring that will be matched against the property value. Use the string values `true` or `false` for boolean properties.

key.description: Use one of the properties the user has provided in the plan.
Use one of the properties the user has provided in the plan.

operator.description: `exact` - exact match of any of the values. `is_not` - does not match any of the values.

value.description: Only use property values from the plan. Always use strings as values. If you have a number, convert it to a string first. If you have a boolean, convert it to a string "true" or "false".

value.description: Value must be a date in ISO 8601 format.

type.description: Filter events by cohort membership. Use this to narrow down results to persons belonging to a specific cohort. Example: `{ type: "cohort", key: "id", value: 42, operator: "in" }`

value.description: The cohort ID to filter by.

key.description: The element property to filter on. `tag_name` — HTML tag (e.g., `button`, `a`, `input`). `text` — visible text content of the element. `href` — the `href` attribute for links. `selector` — a CSS selector matching the element (e.g., `div.main > button.cta`).

operator.description: `icontains` - case insensitive contains. `not_icontains` - case insensitive does not contain. `regex` - matches the regex pattern. `not_regex` - does not match the regex pattern.

type.description: Filter by autocaptured HTML element properties (`$autocapture`, `$rageclick`). Example: `{ type: "element", key: "text", value: "Sign Up", operator: "exact" }`

value.description: Only use property values from the plan. If the operator is `regex` or `not_regex`, the value must be a valid ClickHouse regex pattern to match against. Otherwise, the value must be a substring that will be matched against the property value. Use the string values `true` or `false` for boolean properties.

key.description:
A HogQL boolean expression used as a filter condition.

Examples:
- Filter where a property exceeds a threshold: `toFloat(properties.load_time) > 5.0`
- Filter with string matching: `properties.$current_url LIKE '%/pricing%'`
- Filter with multiple conditions: `properties.$browser = 'Chrome' AND toFloat(properties.duration) > 30`

type.description: Filter by a HogQL boolean expression for advanced filtering that can't be expressed with standard property filters.

key.description: The feature flag key.

type.description:
Filter events by feature flag state — only include events where a specific flag evaluated to a given value. Examples:
- Flag enabled: `{ type: "flag", key: "new-onboarding", operator: "flag_evaluates_to", value: true }`
- Specific variant: `{ type: "flag", key: "checkout-experiment", operator: "flag_evaluates_to", value: "variant-a" }`

value.description: `true`/`false` for boolean flags, or a variant name string for multivariate flags.

retentionFilter.description: Properties specific to the retention insight

aggregationProperty.description: The event or person property to aggregate when aggregationType is sum or avg.

aggregationPropertyType.description: The type of property to aggregate on (event or person). Defaults to event.

aggregationType.description: The aggregation type to use for retention.

cumulative.description: Whether retention should be rolling (aka unbounded, cumulative). Rolling retention means that a user coming back in period 5 makes them count towards all the previous periods.

meanRetentionCalculation.description: Whether an additional series should be shown, showing the mean conversion for each period across cohorts.

minimumOccurrences.description: Minimum number of times an event must occur to count towards retention.

period.description: Retention period, the interval to track cohorts by.

retentionReference.description: Whether retention is with regard to initial cohort size, or that of the previous period.

retentionType.description: Retention type: recurring or first time. Recurring retention counts a user as part of a cohort if they performed the cohort event during that time period, irrespective of it was their first time or not. First time retention only counts a user as part of the cohort if it was their first time performing the cohort event.

custom_name.description: Custom name for the event if it is needed to be renamed.

id.description: The event name from the plan as a string. This is the field the retention query engine uses to match events, so it must be populated exactly as the event appears in the plan. For actions use `AssistantRetentionActionsNode` instead, where `id` is the numeric action ID.

name.description: Optional human-readable label for the event, used for display only. Defaults to `id` if omitted and is never used for event matching.

value.description: Only use property values from the plan. If the operator is `regex` or `not_regex`, the value must be a valid ClickHouse regex pattern to match against. Otherwise, the value must be a substring that will be matched against the property value. Use the string values `true` or `false` for boolean properties.

key.description: The element property to filter on. `tag_name` — HTML tag (e.g., `button`, `a`, `input`). `text` — visible text content of the element. `href` — the `href` attribute for links. `selector` — a CSS selector matching the element (e.g., `div.main > button.cta`).

type.description: Filter by autocaptured HTML element properties (`$autocapture`, `$rageclick`). Example: `{ type: "element", key: "text", value: "Sign Up", operator: "exact" }`

key.description: The element property to filter on. `tag_name` — HTML tag (e.g., `button`, `a`, `input`). `text` — visible text content of the element. `href` — the `href` attribute for links. `selector` — a CSS selector matching the element (e.g., `div.main > button.cta`).

operator.description: `exact` - exact match of any of the values. `is_not` - does not match any of the values.

type.description: Filter by autocaptured HTML element properties (`$autocapture`, `$rageclick`). Example: `{ type: "element", key: "text", value: "Sign Up", operator: "exact" }`

value.description: Only use property values from the plan. Always use strings as values. If you have a number, convert it to a string first. If you have a boolean, convert it to a string "true" or "false".

key.description: The element property to filter on. `tag_name` — HTML tag (e.g., `button`, `a`, `input`). `text` — visible text content of the element. `href` — the `href` attribute for links. `selector` — a CSS selector matching the element (e.g., `div.main > button.cta`).

type.description: Filter by autocaptured HTML element properties (`$autocapture`, `$rageclick`). Example: `{ type: "element", key: "text", value: "Sign Up", operator: "exact" }`

value.description: Value must be a date in ISO 8601 format.

key.description: The element property to filter on. `tag_name` — HTML tag (e.g., `button`, `a`, `input`). `text` — visible text content of the element. `href` — the `href` attribute for links. `selector` — a CSS selector matching the element (e.g., `div.main > button.cta`).

operator.description: `is_set` - the property has any value. `is_not_set` - the property doesn't have a value or wasn't collected.

type.description: Filter by autocaptured HTML element properties (`$autocapture`, `$rageclick`). Example: `{ type: "element", key: "text", value: "Sign Up", operator: "exact" }`

key.description:
A HogQL boolean expression used as a filter condition.

Examples:
- Filter where a property exceeds a threshold: `toFloat(properties.load_time) > 5.0`
- Filter with string matching: `properties.$current_url LIKE '%/pricing%'`
- Filter with multiple conditions: `properties.$browser = 'Chrome' AND toFloat(properties.duration) > 30`

type.description: Filter by a HogQL boolean expression for advanced filtering that can't be expressed with standard property filters.

key.description: The feature flag key.

type.description:
Filter events by feature flag state — only include events where a specific flag evaluated to a given value. Examples:
- Flag enabled: `{ type: "flag", key: "new-onboarding", operator: "flag_evaluates_to", value: true }`
- Specific variant: `{ type: "flag", key: "checkout-experiment", operator: "flag_evaluates_to", value: "variant-a" }`

value.description: `true`/`false` for boolean flags, or a variant name string for multivariate flags.

id.description: The numeric action ID from the plan. This is the field the retention query engine uses to look up the action definition. For events use `AssistantRetentionEventsNode` instead, where `id` is the event name string.

name.description: Optional human-readable label for the action, used for display only. Defaults to the action's stored name if omitted and is never used for action matching.

key.description: Use one of the properties the user has provided in the plan.

Otherwise, the value must be a substring that will be matched against the property value. Use the string values `true` or `false` for boolean properties.

type.description: Filter events by cohort membership. Use this to narrow down results to persons belonging to a specific cohort. Example: `{ type: "cohort", key: "id", value: 42, operator: "in" }`

targetEntity.description: Activation event (event putting the actor into the initial cohort).

timeWindowMode.description: The time window mode to use for retention calculations.

totalIntervals.description: How many intervals to show in the chart. The default value is 8 (meaning 7 periods after initial cohort).
